creator: will@physics.purdue.edu
text:
On Tue, Aug 28, 2001 at 09:51:45PM +0200, Thierry Thomas (thierry@thomas.as) wrote:
> >Synopsis:       Impossible to fetch KDE2 from a machine running KDE1
> 	
> >Description:
> 	I was trying to make fetch-recursive KDE2 from a machine where only KDE1 is installed (to upgrade anothermachine where KDE2.1 is installed), but it stopped into kdelibs, due to the following code:
> 
> .if exists(${LOCALBASE}/include/kfm.h)
>         @${ECHO}
>         @${ECHO} "                      NOTICE"
>         @${ECHO}
>         @${ECHO} "You have KDE1 headers installed!  Installing this port"
>         @${ECHO} "will result in conflicts between KDE2 and KDE1!"
>         @${ECHO}   
>         @${FALSE}
> .endif

I'll move the failure notice to pre-extract target.  Thanks for
the heads-up.

-- 
wca
